prompt: Grill me on the system design for real-time collaborative cursors and write it up. Blueprint is collab-cursors-blueprint.md in this folder. We've got a Node backend that's REST-only today, Postgres, React frontend.
assertions: The document is complete to the skill's template: all 15 sections are present and filled, and the Decision Coverage Checklist accounts for every one of the 22 taxonomy dimensions (A1-A14 and B1-B8) with a status (Resolved / Assumed / N/A) and a substantive note — no row left blank or rubber-stamped.
Assumptions and Accepted Compromises are each first-class tables (not prose); every accepted compromise names the specific design principle it bends and gives a concrete revisit trigger.

The design selects a real-time transport (WebSocket or SSE) over the existing REST-only backend and justifies the choice specifically against the blueprint's sub-250ms perceived-latency requirement with actual reasoning, not just an assertion.

Cursor/presence state is treated as ephemeral — explicitly NOT persisted to the document store (Postgres) — and the design names where transient presence state lives instead. (Correctness trap: persisting cursors to the main DB.)

The design handles graceful degradation when the presence service is unavailable: the document still opens and is fully editable with no error blocking the editor — matching the blueprint deviation and applying 'design for failure'.
The design defends against the abuse scenario of one client flooding cursor updates — it bounds or throttles the update rate (backpressure) so a flood does not degrade other participants.

Cursor identity (name and color) is assigned or validated server-side so a client cannot spoof another user, and cursor data is delivered only for documents the user is authorized to open — both adversarial scenarios.

The design handles out-of-order cursor updates (only the latest position per user is shown) and unclean-disconnect detection, and it identifies the central trade-off — adding an always-on transport and a presence tier to a REST-only system — with principle-grounded reasoning.
